channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: New Kingdom Come: Deliverance Game Can Be Pre-Ordered, But It’s $199.99
item.content:encoded:
There’s a whole new Kingdom Come: Deliverance game coming out soon, and you can pre-order it now. The only catch is, it’ll set you back a mighty $199.99. It’s Kingdom Come: Deliverance The Board Game, and it’s an all-new creation from Czech Games Edition in partnership with Warhorse Studios.

It might sound steep, but for fans of the franchise and tabletop aficionados, there’s plenty under the hood (or in the box) for this game. For starters, it’s estimated that the running time stacks up to around 90 minutes per player.

Kingdom Come: Deliverance The Board Game Revealed

In a post on Instagram, Czech Games Edition unveiled the incoming Kingdom Come board game, helpfully named Kingdom Come: Deliverance The Board Game. It has been pieced together by award-winning veterans, Vlaada Chuátil and Tomás Holek, the former of whom has more than 40 games to his name and a place in the Origins Award Hall of Fame.

The board game, which is still in development, comprises a stunning collection of components. There’s a fully illustrated map of the Sázava region, pulled from the first game that debuted in 2018. There are 700 cards, including skills, encounters, traits, equipment, quests, potions, and treasures, four player boards, horse tiles, 180+ tokens, and much, much more.

It’s one of the deepest and full-fledged board games I’ve seen in a hot minute, that’s for sure.

It’s a multi-dimensional, multi-faceted thing with twisting storylines, side quests, and ways to play. There are more than 200 wood tokens, an ‘intuitive’ storage solution, stands, boxes, and even a solo mode, for those who want to play alone.

  • RELATED: New Kingdom Come Game Could Launch Next Year

You have the option to bolt on a bundle of replica metal ‘groschen’ coins and a pack of card sleeves, if that’s your speed. If you pre-order, you’ll get an exclusive promo quest, a double-sided time track, and a day track, all as bonus 3D-printed tokens.

One hell of an effort has gone into bringing Kingdom Come: Deliverance The Board Game to life. When you pre-order, you’ll be told that the item will ship around October 2026, but certain regions will have to either wait a little longer or go to a specific location to pick up their copy of the game.

Are you interested in the Kingdom Come: Deliverance board game? Let us know what you think about it on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Thick As Thieves Developer OtherSide Entertainment Lays Off 18 People
item.content:encoded:
For the second time in a month, Thick as Thieves developer OtherSide Entertainment is laying off over a dozen people.

First reported by Game Developer, the Boston, Massachusetts-based studio is letting go of 18 more employees. This follows the 17 people who were let go earlier this month.

Current Company Makeup Wasn’t “Commercially Viable”

In a statement given to Game Developer, a spokesperson for OtherSide Entertainment confirmed the layoffs. The spokesperson also said that the studio’s development of immersive sim games has been “an increasingly challenging space to occupy.”

“OtherSide’s DNA is built on immersive sim games that have lengthy development cycles,” the company said. “This, unfortunately, has been an increasingly challenging space to occupy in recent years. Despite the encouraging response to the launch of Thick as Thieves’ introductory campaign, it has become clear that the continuation of the studio in its current shape is no longer a commercially viable path.”

The company added that it was “deeply grateful” to the staff for the work they’ve done, and that it regrets having to make the decision it did.

Thick as Thieves launched on May 20 and peaked with over 1,100 concurrent players on Steam. Since then, however, the stealth-action heist game has seen its player totals fall, averaging just over 100 concurrents per day.

What do you think the future holds for Thick as Thieves and developer OtherSide Entertainment?  Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, check out this exclusive first look at a new feature coming to Backyard Baseball when it launches on PC on July 9. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: July’s PlayStation Plus Monthly Games Have Been Revealed
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation Plus will offer three monthly games to Essential players in July, and Modern Warfare 3 is one of them.

The latest announcement regarding PlayStation Plus games followed shortly after Sony shared significant news with players: the company will cease physical disc releases for games launched after January 28. It seems that, to appease players after this announcement, Sony is including a substantial release in the July PS Plus monthly games lineup.

PlayStation Plus Monthly Games for July 2026 Features Modern Warfare 3 and 2 More Titles

The new blog post from Sony confirms that July’s PlayStation Plus monthly games will be free to claim between July 7 and August 3, 2026. The first entry is the big one, Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3 – Cross-Gen Bundle for PS5 and PS4. Then players also get For The King 2 for PS5 & PS4 and CrossCode for PS5 & PS4 in July. Players have until July 6 to add the June 2026 PS Plus monthly games to their libraries.

  • More Insider Gaming: PlayStation’s No Disc Plans for 2028 Could Hint at PS6 Release Date

Modern Warfare 3 will feature a brand-new campaign that includes modernized versions of classic multiplayer maps, as well as an open-world PvE Zombies experience. For The King 2 is a tabletop adventure RPG in which you and your four-player party will journey through Fahrul to confront the tyrannical queen and put an end to her oppressive reign.CrossCode is a retro-inspired 2D action RPG with 16-bit-style graphics. Players will enjoy smooth physics, a fast-paced combat system, and engaging puzzle mechanics, all set within a captivating sci-fi story.

What are your thoughts on PlayStation Plus monthly games for July 2026? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Subnautica CEO resigning as Krafton agrees to pay compensation. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Esports Nations Cup: PUBG Rosters and Invited Teams Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
The Esports Foundation has revealed the official team rosters and invites for the PUBG portion of the upcoming international event, poised to take place in November in Saudi Arabia. It’s one of the most ambitious esports tournaments in history, and it’s set to identify which country is truly the best when it comes to competitive gaming.

It was confirmed that a whopping 80 rosters representing as many countries submitted their application for the PUBG tournament at the Esports Nations Cup 2026, but only 24 squads will make it to the final at the ENC.

All Players Invited to PUBG at the Esports Nations Cup

The Esports Nations Cup will follow the Esports World Cup this year, the latter of which was moved to Paris just weeks ago. It has proven to be a shrewd relocation, as the fans are gathering and the ceremonies are shaping up quite nicely.

In a new press release, the Esports Foundation revealed the spread of PUBG rosters competing at the Esports Nations Cup this coming November:

  1. THAILAND – Nourinz, Lericz, Scappy, TiGGER, Belmoth, KiSS
  2. PEOPLE’S REPUBLIC OF CHINA – MMing, 04NB, LilGhost, tiantian, HS
  3. REPUBLIC OF KOREA – EENd, Heather, Heaven, Gyumin, seoul, Seongjang
  4. VIETNAM – Himass, Delwyn, TanVuu, YmCuD, Zest, DjChip
  5. UKRAINE – Feyerist, Hakatory, SoseD, DiFX, Hexecu1or, Drugomu
  6. UNITED STATES OF AMERICA – Purdy, Shrimzy, Kickstart, CowBoi, diyy, SneakAttack
  7. CROATIA – Lukarux, NIXZYEE, slqven, Nod1, ivas, ZEKO
  8. DENMARK – Beami, Gustav, Ketter, Mikzenn, Doffy, BAAAD3
  9. FINLAND – curexi, SkaV, S4TTA, iExE, stratos, Mise
  10. BRAZIL – zkrakenN, sparkingg, possa, cauan7zin, Tny7, fana
  11. AUSTRALIA – TGLTN, luke12, Fludd, Monty, Ripp, Dusky
  12. ARGENTINA – Caspitan, emikxz, PIPAA, Dr4FTk1NG, Kalnixx, SzylzEN

These are the twelve invited teams that have earned those qualifications by placing high in tournaments in official PUBG esports events around the world. Most of the competitors invited were on the stage during the recent PUBG Nations Cup, a similarly named tournament that pulled in record viewership figures.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

The PUBG portion of the Esports Nations Cup is expected to be one of the most energetic tournaments during the entire showcase. Are you looking forward to it? Let us know your predictions on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that the COD Champs bracket is locked in, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Subnautica CEO Resigns as Krafton Agrees to Pay ‘Significant’ Compensation to Entire Staff
item.content:encoded:
In an all-new report, it has been confirmed that Krafton has agreed to pay extensive bonuses to the staff at Unknown Worlds Entertainment, bringing an end to a months-long financial dispute. The legal issues involved a lawsuit filed against the studio’s leadership team, based on claims that they’d ‘abandoned’ their management duties.

The situation stemmed from Krafton’s original $500 million purchase of Unknown Worlds, which took place in 2021. There was an additional $250 million promised in bonuses to the company if Subnautica 2, then in development, was a success.

Today, it has been confirmed that Krafton has finally settled on that payout, and Unknown Worlds’ CEO, Ted Gill, has resigned.

Unknown Worlds Entertainment Situation is Settled

Bloomberg’s Jason Schreier confirmed that Ted Gill, CEO at Unknown Worlds, the studio behind Subnautica, has resigned. Last year, Gill was dismissed by Krafton as the financial and legal concerns escalated. The situation became even more convoluted when Gill was reinstated as the CEO in March 2026.

Speaking to Bloomberg, Gill explained his departure:

We mutually agreed to part ways. New leadership is the best way for the studio to move forward.

Gill also reported that Krafton’s payout to the staff of Unknown Worlds Entertainment will be ‘significantly more’ than originally intended, and that staff will receive ‘further incentives’ as they keep working on Subnautica 2.

Subnautica 2 was released in early access on May 14th, and it quickly soared up the charts with unimaginable power. On Steam alone, it hit almost half a million concurrent players within a matter of hours, racking up tens of thousands of positive reviews.

Per the details published by Bloomberg, every employee at Unknown Worlds will receive a portion of the bonus, including staff who might have only joined recently.

  • RELATED: What Will Be Improved in Subnautica 2

Now that this issue seems to have been put to bed, it effectively brings to a close several months of litigation and drama.

Did you give Subnautica 2 a try yet? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter
